ddls:
CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the model (e.g., Naive Bayes, SVM) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model and its purpose */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the model was registered */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the model */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the model */,
  "hyperparameters" TEXT /* JSON field to store the hyperparameters used for the model */,
  "source_code" TEXT /* Text field to store the source code or a reference to the source code of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of the dataset (e.g., Image Segments, Text Data) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset and its content */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the dataset was registered */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the dataset */,
  "size_mb" REAL /* Size of the dataset in megabytes */,
  "format" TEXT /* Format of the dataset (e.g., CSV, JSON, Parquet) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the dataset (e.g., internal, external, public repository) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being evaluated */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset used for evaluation */,
  "step" INTEGER /* Step number in the evaluation process */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the model at this step */,
  "micro_f1" REAL /* Micro F1 score of the model */,
  "macro_f1" REAL /* Macro F1 score of the model */,
  "memory_mb" REAL /* Memory used during evaluation in MB */,
  "time_s" REAL /* Time taken for evaluation in seconds */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the performance metric was logged */,
  "run_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the specific run of the model evaluation */,
  "additional_metrics" TEXT /* JSON field to store additional performance metrics not covered by the existing columns */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the evaluation step (e.g., completed, failed, running) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data scientist, engineer, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user account was created */,
  "last_login_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last login of the user */,
  "last_activity_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last activity of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., Data Science, Engineering) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the user account is active */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON field to store specific permissions or roles of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tasks" (
  "task_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each task */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model associated with the task */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the task */,
  "task_type" TEXT /* Type of task performed (e.g., training, evaluation) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the task was performed */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the task was created */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the task (e.g., pending, in progress, completed, failed) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the task */,
  "additional_info" TEXT /* JSON field to store additional information related to the task */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tasks_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tasks_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
